themes.reasoning_notes: Scavenge turns it from graveyard into a large burst of +1/+1 counters, directly supporting counters decks.
A 4-mana 5/5 is an efficient standalone body, and later scavenge lets it convert into more board pressure.
Scavenge uses the graveyard as a resource, but it is not true recursion since the card exiles itself instead of returning.
Scavenge converts it in the graveyard into a one-shot pile of +1/+1 counters, a clear counters enabler rather than a counters payoff.
A vanilla 5/5 for four is solid standalone board presence, and scavenge preserves late-game pressure after it dies.
